2026-03-06.content: Word in History: How the Canon Came to Be - Wesley Huff

2026-03-06.html: Lord in History: How Jesus' Divinity Shaped Our World - Andy Steiger, Ph.D

2026-03-06.title: Panel Q&A with Andy Steiger, Wesley Huff (Moderated by Steve Kim)

2026-03-07.title: Doors open. Complimentary coffee/tea and COBS scone (1 COBS/person).

2026-03-07.content: Witness in History: Bullies & Saints, an Honeset Reckoning - John Dickson, Ph.D
Nature in History: How Christianity Sparked the Birth of Modern Science - Nancy Pearcey
Q&A with John Dickson and Nancy Pearcey (Moderated by Steve Kim)
Orthodoxy in History: Defining the Boundaries of Belief - Ben MacDonald, Ph.D

speakers.title: President
speakers.bio:
Dr. Andy Steiger is the founder and president of Apologetics Canada Ministries (AC). He passionately engages pressing issues related to our humanity from a Christian perspective. He has presented at various venues such as Cambridge University on philosophical issues related to Artificial Intelligence, provided ethical guidelines on robotics for the United Nations, and addressed legal concerns over rights of conscience at the World Congress in Philosophy of Law.

Andy has published widely from children’s books to the Supreme Court Law Review.

Between his 20 years of pastoral experience and a PhD from the University of Aberdeen, Andy brings both experience and research to his work.

Andy currently lives in Abbotsford, British Columbia, with his wife, Nancy, and their two sons. Most weekends you’ll find the Steigers in the mountains or lakes of BC.

sessions.title: Lord in History
sessions.subtitle: How Jesus' Divinity Shaped Our World
sessions.description: Early Christians insisted that the divinity of Jesus entered real history, not myth. That’s why the creeds anchor the Incarnation under a Roman governor—pinning eternity to a date on the calendar. It testifies that God’s self-revelation took on the empire and law courts. The creed isn’t just theology; it is a historical declaration that Jesus is the true King.

sessions.title: Missing Books?
sessions.subtitle: Why Protestants Don't Include the Apocrypha as Inspired Scripture
sessions.description: How many books are in the Bible? The answer depends on whom you ask: Roman Catholics typically say 73, Eastern Orthodox Christians 76 (sometimes 81), and Protestants 66. Why the difference? It largely turns on a collection of writings commonly called the Old Testament Apocrypha—often called 'deuterocanonical books' in the Roman Catholic tradition. Their status has been debated for centuries: Are they helpful history and wisdom literature, or should they be received as inspired Scripture? In this session, Andy Steiger will explain what the Apocrypha is, why the canon question matters, and offer seven reasons Protestants have historically not included these books in the Bible.

speakers.title: Vice President
speakers.bio:
Wesley Huff was born in Multan, Pakistan, and spent a portion of his childhood in the Middle East. After being diagnosed with a rare neurological condition at the age of eleven that left him paralyzed from the waist down, Wes experienced a miraculous recovery for which the doctors themselves had no medical explanation. This experience, along with a great deal of study and soul searching in his later teen years, solidified his interest in faith and in engaging the beliefs and worldview perspectives of others. He spent his high school years in Northern Ontario and currently lives in Toronto, Canada, with his wife and four children.

Wesley is currently the Vice President for Apologetics Canada and has been a guest on a variety of public platforms, from The Joe Rogan Experience to Blurry Creatures Podcast. He has participated in numerous public dialogues, debates, and interfaith events on issues of belief and religion across North America.

He holds a BA in sociology from York University, a Masters of Theological Studies from Tyndale University, and is currently doing a PhD in New Testament at the University of Toronto’s Wycliffe College.

sessions.title: Word in History
sessions.subtitle: How the Canon Came to Be
sessions.description: The Bible did not drop from heaven leather-bound; it formed as the people of God recognized the voice of their Lord across centuries. Councils, communities, and controversies all served a providential process of reception, not mere invention. Human discernment was real, yet God remained the Author guiding the canon’s recognition. This talk traces the criteria, conflicts, and consensus that congealed into Scripture. The result is a book both embedded in history and transcending it.

speakers.title: Historian, Speaker
speakers.bio:
John’s story is eclectic. Starting out as a professional singer-songwriter, he now works as an author, speaker, historian, and media presenter. He was the Founding Director of the Centre for Public Christianity (2007-17). He has published over 20 books, two of which became television documentaries, with a third, For the Love of God: How the Church is Better and Worse Than you Ever Imagined, released in Australian cinemas in June 2018.

John has held a variety of teaching and research positions before moving to Wheaton College, including in the Ancient History Department at Macquarie University (2002-2015), the Jewish and Biblical Studies Department at Sydney University (2011-2021), Ridley College Melbourne (2019-2022), and the Faculty of Classics at the University of Oxford (2015-2023). A busy public speaker, he lives in Wheaton, Illinois, with his wife Elizabeth and the youngest of their three children.

Head to johndickson.org for more on John’s speaking and writing activities.

sessions.title: Witness in History
sessions.subtitle: Bullies & Saints, an Honest Reckoning
sessions.description: This session takes a candid tour through the church’s brightest witness and darkest failures—martyrs and missionaries alongside inquisitors, slavers, and abusers. We’ll ask what counted as “faithful” in its time, how power, culture, and fear distorted good intentions, and where repentance and reform broke through. Using case studies across eras, the session models fair-minded assessment: naming harm without cynicism and honoring holiness without hagiography. We’ll trace recurring patterns—revival and drift, courage and compromise—and the checks that helped (Scripture, conscience, lay movements, accountability). You’ll leave with a simple toolkit for historical discernment (sources, context, criteria) and a pastoral posture for today: truthful about sin, grateful for grace, and hopeful about reform.

sessions.title: How Christianity Won the World
sessions.description: In this session, Dr Dickson explores key factors in the extraordinary growth of Christianity in the pre-Christian Roman world and draws out lessons for our own mission to the post-Christian world.

speakers.title: Professor of Apologetics
speakers.bio:
Nancy Pearcey studied under Francis Schaeffer at L’Abri in the early 1970s, and after earning an MA did additional graduate work at the Institute for Christian Studies in Toronto. She has taught at Biola (Visiting Scholar, Torrey Honors Institute), World Journalism Institute (Francis Schaeffer Scholar), and Carin University (Professor and Scholar of Worldview Studies). She has been an invited speaker at dozens of Christian seminaries, and on campuses such as USC, UC Davis, UC Santa Barbara, the University of Georgia, Dartmouth, Stanford, and Princeton.

Hailed in The Economist as “America’s pre-eminent evangelical Protestant female intellectual,” Pearcey has spoken to actors and screenwriters in Hollywood; to scientists at Sandia and Los Alamos National Laboratories; to staffers on Capitol Hill and the White House; and to think tanks and public policy groups such as the Family Research Council, Faith & Law, Concerned Women for America, Eagle Forum, Council for National Policy, the Constitutional Coalition, and the Heritage Foundation. She is currently a fellow at the Discovery Institute’s Center for Science and Culture.

As the founding and executive editor of BreakPoint radio, Pearcey wrote 1000+ radio commentaries and edited thousands more for broadcast. A former commentator on Public Square Radio, Pearcey has made more than 300 media appearances, including Hot Air TV, Bill Bennett Show, Dennis Miller Show, the Dennis Prager Show, NPR, Fox & Friends, Focus on the Family, Breitbart and C-SPAN’s Book TV, and The Mike Huckabee Show.

sessions.title: Nature in History
sessions.subtitle: How Christianity Sparked the Birth of Modern Science
sessions.description: The most common reason given in surveys for leaving religion behind is that it contradicts science. Yet historians have debunked the stereotype of “warfare” between science and religion. Nancy Pearcey shows how to make a persuasive case that the Christian worldview provided fundamental principles that were necessary for the rise of modern science—and that continue to provide the foundation for the scientific enterprise today.

sessions.title: How the Script for Masculinity Turned Toxic—and How to Fix It
sessions.description: We often hear that evangelical Christian men are Exhibit A of toxic masculinity—that belief in male headship turns them into overbearing, tyrannical patriarchs. But the social sciences tell a very different story. Studies find that Christian family men who attend church regularly test out as the most loving husbands and the most engaged fathers. They have the lowest rate of divorce and, surprisingly, they have the lowest rate of domestic violence of any group in America. International studies likewise give evidence that Christianity has the power to overcome toxic behaviour and reconcile the sexes.

speakers.bio:
Dr. Ben MacDonald is a theologian, speaker, and educator with a heart for engaging faith and culture. He lives in Halifax, Nova Scotia, with his wife Melissa and their daughter Josephine. Ben holds two undergraduate degrees in English Language & Literature from the University of Western Ontario and a Bachelor of Journalism from the University of King’s College. After a brief career in television news, Ben earned his Master of Theology from Acadia Divinity College and completed a PhD in Theological Studies at Wycliffe College, TST.

In addition to his work with Apologetics Canada, Ben serves as recruiter and adjunct lecturer at Acadia Divinity College. He has spent several years as a travelling speaker and preacher, teaching theology to congregations across the Maritimes and Ontario. With a deep love for both the Church and the academy, Ben is committed to equipping others to think deeply about their faith and engage thoughtfully with the world around them.

sessions.title: Orthodoxy in History
sessions.subtitle: Defining Boundaries of Belief
sessions.description: This session traces how Christians have marked the edges of faithful confession from the apostles to the present. We’ll follow the story from rule of faith and early creeds to the ecumenical councils, showing how debates over Christ, Scripture, and salvation clarified the center by naming the edges. Along the way, we’ll examine why some teachings were judged heretical—Arianism, Gnosticism, and others—not as power plays alone but as safeguards for the gospel’s coherence and worship’s integrity. The session highlights the difference between development and distortion, drawing on guides like the Vincentian canon (“everywhere, always, by all”) and the pastoral aims behind doctrinal boundaries. We’ll also explore how political pressures, cultural languages, and local church life shaped outcomes—revealing both the human messiness and the providential wisdom of the process. Participants will leave with a practical framework for navigating contested issues today: how to major on the majors, name real red lines, and practice charity without surrendering clarity.

sessions.title: The Triune God
sessions.subtitle: Unity, Persons, and the Life of Salvation
sessions.description: We often hear that evangelical Christian men are Exhibit A of toxic masculinity—that belief in male headship turns them into overbearing, tyrannical patriarchs. But the social sciences tell a very different story. Studies find that Christian family men who attend church regularly test out as the most loving husbands and the most engaged fathers. They have the lowest rate of divorce and, surprisingly, they have the lowest rate of domestic violence of any group in America. International studies likewise give evidence that Christianity has the power to overcome toxic behaviour and reconcile the sexes.

speakers.title: Teacher, Therapist
speakers.bio: Diana has always been curious about what helps living things grow and thrive, first in the natural world and now in family systems, both designed to reflect the Creator’s glory. She holds Bachelor of Science in Natural Resource Conservation and a Bachelor of Education from the University of British Columbia, and is currently completing a Master of Arts in Couple and Family Therapy at Briercrest Seminary. Drawing on her experience as a teacher, parent, and therapist, she is passionate about helping families build resilience and navigate challenges with discernment and faith. Her current research explores a systems-based understanding of how families and children can flourish in today’s technological and cultural climate. Diana is grateful to serve as the children’s ministry coordinator at her local church and to homeschool her children. She lives with her husband Beni and children Alice and Caleb on a Christmas tree farm in Langley, British Columbia, where she enjoys gardening, knitting, and reading books on obscure history, most often with a cup of coffee in hand.

sessions.title: Raising Resilience
sessions.subtitle: Strengthening Families in the Age of Technology
sessions.description: Adolescents today are navigating developmental milestones within an ecosystem saturated in technology. From social media to video games to smartphones, technology is inescapable. Research has shown that when the use of technology becomes problematic, it impacts adolescents’ ability to adapt and thrive in the face of adversity - in other words, to become resilient adults. The family system was designed by God to protect and disciple children. This session will explore what factors within the family system can help adolescents cultivate the emotional, relational, and spiritual resilience needed to flourish in an increasingly digital world.

speakers.title: Biblical Counsellor, Pro-Life Advocate
speakers.bio: Bronwyn Gray is a deeply loved daughter of God, very precious in His sight. This is the most important thing about her. She has also taught the high school Bible and English for over six years, two of which were in Cambodia. She is blessed to be a certified biblical counselor and hold an undergraduate degree in Bible and education in addition to a graduate degree in English. Her master's thesis involved research on a biblical understanding of human value which was life-changing and inspired her to write a script and direct a short pro-life film called Worth More, addressing the critical issue of euthanasia in Canada. Since the film's release, she has spoken to hundreds of people in B.C., Alberta, and Ontario on the value of every human life, collaborating with several amazing pro-life organizations like ARPA Canada, Campaign Life Coalition, Compassionate Community Care, Delta Hospice Society, and more. She is passionate that people experience the freedom that comes from knowing their intrinsic worth and that the truth about 'MAiD' be exposed in order for the elderly and people with disabilities to be protected and valued, and in the hope that there will be a decline in the horrifying numbers of precious human beings choosing to be euthanized.

sessions.title: The Psychological Impact of 'MAiD'
sessions.subtitle: Another Great Reason NOT to Succumb
sessions.description: 'After the procedure had happened, it took years for me to stop having panic attacks, and my husband often had to wake me up in the middle of my night terrors to get me to stop screaming. I mostly don’t remember the nightmares, but there are a few that stick with me.' This is what one BC woman shared with Bronwyn after, out of respect for her father, she attended his death by euthanasia. Is this a normal response when a beloved family member chooses to die by euthanasia? Based on the popular cultural narrative, it is disrespectful to question a family member or friend's decision to choose euthanasia. We are told that this is a person's private choice, and family members don't need to be legally informed. But does this trajectory fit with what it means to be human? How might over 90,000 people choosing to be euthanized affect our nation's morale? Come learn about parallels and differences between the effects of suicide on a family and the effects of euthanasia and consider how you can use this powerful argument to dissuade people from MAiD, by reminding them that their choices always affect others because of our fundamental interconnectedness as humans.

speakers.title: Evangelist, Co-Founder
speakers.bio:
Growing up, I was hurt badly by the church – multiple times. I feared each weekend knowing I'd face constant judgment, gossip, and was forced into legalistic routines. My breaking point was a weekend retreat where several Christians forced me to sit down and I received relentless criticism for hours all through the night, letting me know everything that was wrong with me until the sun started to rise. I remember never wanting anything to do with church after that weekend.

God is good however, and two evangelists found me at the perfect time when I was at the end of myself. They introduced the love of Christ and helped restore my broken perception of Jesus. They explained that if someone plays a beautiful Beethoven piece terribly, you don't blame Beethoven, you blame the flawed pianist. The same way, Jesus is perfect, but His followers can be so broken.

So through a series of supernatural events, my heart transformed and I fell madly in love with Jesus. While I am a small, feeble man, I serve a great and powerful God who set me on a mission to share this good news. I can’t wait to share God’s transformative work in my life with you.

Jee-Woon is the co-founder of J’s Kitchen, a homeless ministry in Vancouver, BC, focused on seeing displaced lives restored through meeting physical and spiritual needs. Outside of the ministry, Jee-Woon is a Personal Finance Instructor at various institutions, a Financial Advisor for faith-based financial planning, and a deacon at Evangelical Chinese Bible Church. He enjoys writing music, playing sports, and is getting married in March to his fiancée, Sarah.

sessions.title: Everyday Evangelism
sessions.subtitle: Practical Ways to Lead Friends and Strangers to Christ
sessions.description:
'I don’t know how to start or what to say.'
'What if I say the wrong thing?'
'Evangelism isn’t my gift; it’s for pastors and missionaries.'

If you’ve ever thought this way, you’re not alone. Jee-Woon once felt the same hesitation until he discovered that evangelism isn’t about eloquence or expertise, but about radical hospitality, genuine compassion and obedience. Evangelism and apologetics isn’t about winning arguments, it’s about winning souls for Christ.

Join Jee-Woon as he shares stories on how God transformed his passive attitude to gradually discover how everyday moments can turn into gospel encounters. You’ll discover practical ways to share your faith with friends, family, and strangers from all backgrounds, and even how a change of heart led Jee-Woon to co-founding a homeless ministry that has since served over a thousand meals, helped individuals overcome addiction, and witnessed many displaced lives give their life to Christ.

You may be the only authentic Christian influence someone meets today—will you take the opportunity to share the good news?

speakers.title: Pastor, Speaker
speakers.bio:
Greg Harris has served in pastoral ministry since 2010, and has been on the pastoral team at SouthRidge Fellowship since Fall 2022. He believes the church should be a place of mutuality, and he desires to see each disciple of Jesus—regardless of their dis/abilities—mature in Christlikeness.

Greg received a Dip. in Biblical Studies and a B.A. in Worship Arts from Columbia Bible College; an MDiv from Northwest Baptist Seminary, and is a Doctoral candidate in Practical Theology at McMaster Divinity College (with a research focus on the intersection of dis/ability and discipleship in the local church).

sessions.title: Eugenics and the Local Church:
sessions.subtitle: The Apostle Paul's Critique of Ableism
sessions.description: In light of the humanitarian atrocities of the 20th century—including but not limited to The Holocaust—Christians have historically been leery of the implications of eugenics and its 'promises' of ending that which is 'undesirable' in humanity. This session applies the Christian impulse of opposing eugenics on the human body to the temptation within the North American Evangelical church to impose eugenics on the body of Christ, the local church. Through engagement with the Apostle Paul's Corinthian discourse, this session will make a case for the full belonging and co-labouring of persons of all dis/abilities within the body of Christ in order for Christ's 'power to be made perfect' (2 Cor.12:8) in the local church.

speakers.title: Alberta Director
speakers.bio:
Steve is a follower of Christ with a heart for apologetics which he views as the “intellectual care of the soul.” In his early 20s, he experienced a faith crisis due to intellectual undernourishment. Through this experience, he has come to see apologetics as the “intellectual care of the soul” and now feels a personal burden to walk with others who may be struggling with doubt and/or seeking sincerely. Steve holds a diploma in Worship Arts and a BA in Biblical Studies from Columbia Bible College in Abbotsford, BC. He has completed a master’s degree in Christian Apologetics through Biola University in La Mirada, CA. He began his PhD studies through the University of the Highlands and Islands in Scotland, exploring any proper limits of human enhancement through a dialogue between Friedrich Nietzsche and G. K. Chesterton.

A native of Korea, Steve now lives in the Edmonton area with his wife, Sharina, and two children, Maya and Tavin. His guilty pleasure includes eating instant ramen noodles late at night and watching YouTube videos on North Korea. (What is Kim Jong-Un up to this time?)

sessions.title: Designing the Human Future
sessions.subtitle: The Legacy of Eugenics in Transhumanism
sessions.description:
Transhumanism is the view that we should use technology to tinker with our body and mind to become something better than human. From brain-computer interface to genetic engineering techniques such as CRISPR-Cas9, modern technology certainly seems to be paving the way.

However, while the technology may be more sophisticated, the impulse to improve human conditions fundamentally is an old one. We have witnessed a horrific iteration of this in the 20th century—eugenics. Transhumanists recognize that their endeavour stands in the long shadow of this darkest chapter in the history of medicine. How do they seek to set themselves apart, and is their attempt successful? Join Steve to find out more.

speakers.title: Creative Director
speakers.bio:
Troy Lydiate is a multifaceted artist, speaker, and Creative Director at Apologetics Canada, where he also serves as host of 'The AC Podcast'. As a graduate of Providence University with a Bachelor's Degree focused on Social Sciences, much of Troy's professional career has been spent working with youth and young adults. Operating under the artistic moniker Scribe Music, Troy has unveiled four studio albums, skillfully utilizing his music as a conduit to convey the gospel message. His music production has garnered him nominations at the GMA (Covenant Awards), further complemented by widespread recognition through blog features and regular airplay on Canadian Christian radio.

Beyond his artistic endeavours, Troy is a devoted husband to Jasmine and a doting father to two young children. He has dedicated himself to the growth and vitality of the local church, actively engaging in discipleship, worship team, and church leadership. Embracing a mission to catalyze both cultural and global transformation, Troy's approach is firmly anchored in love, releasing hope by loving God and loving people.

sessions.title: Burn Your Idols
sessions.subtitle: When the Church's Admiration Becomes Adoration
sessions.description:
Every generation of believers faces the same subtle temptation - to elevate God’s messengers above the God they proclaim. In this talk, Troy Lydiate invites the Church to examine its heart and its heroes, exploring how admiration can quietly become adoration, and how love for the “bride of Christ” can sometimes eclipse love for the Bridegroom Himself.

Drawing from biblical accounts of idol worship both ancient and modern, Troy unpacks how good things become god-things, how charisma can outshine character, and how the Church’s longing for leadership can drift into misplaced loyalty. With examples from Scripture and church history, from Moses to modern ministers, this message calls us back to a purified devotion: to burn what’s been built on ego, and rebuild on the foundation of Christ alone.

Because the Church doesn’t need more celebrities. It needs more surrender.

speakers.title: Executive Assistant, Speaker
speakers.bio: Lindsay is a follower of Jesus with a heart for people and the Church. She has served for over ten years in administrative roles that have afforded her the flexibility to engage in ministry, raise two beautiful kids and work toward completing an undergraduate degree in History. Having recently graduated, she is excited to have the opportunity at Apologetics Canada to combine her experience in administration with her passion for furthering God’s Kingdom through encouraging Christians to have confidence in their faith. She lives in Surrey, BC with her donut-enthusiast husband Ajay Martin and their two boisterous kids: Chloe and Obadiah.

sessions.title: Owning Your 'I Don’t Know'
sessions.subtitle: Humble Evangelism Amid Secular Assumptions
sessions.description: There is no shortage of blemishes on the Church's reputation throughout world history, and for the Christian student of history, this comes with some significant challenges. How can we engage honestly with topics like colonialism and the European Witch Trials of the past, while still contending for the goodness and trustworthiness of God in the present? In this session, Lindsay will share about her recent experience in the secular university, and provide four practical guidelines for navigating these challenging spaces with both humility and boldness.

speakers.title: Writer & Illustrator
speakers.bio:
Josh Nadeau is a writer and illustrator from West Coast Canada. He is husband to Aislinn, father to Ransom and Cassian. He has his undergraduate in Physics, his masters in theological studies, and a doctorate from the school of hard knocks.

Follow him on Instagram (@swordandpencil) and check out his website (https://sword-and-pencil.myshopify.com/) to learn more about his work.

sessions.title: Beauty as an Apologetics
sessions.subtitle: How Art and Storytelling Participates in the Healing of the World
sessions.description: In a world starved for meaning and dulled by utility, Beauty remains the oldest and most compelling Christian apologetic. This talk explores how art and storytelling reveal the Beauty of Christ to reach the heart in ways argument never can. Josh invites listeners to become participate in the healing of souls, culture, and the world itself through art and story.

speakers.title: Bible Teacher
speakers.bio: Dr. John Neufeld is the Bible teacher for Back to the Bible Canada, where for over a decade he's been opening Scripture with clarity and conviction. With 50 years of ministry experience as a pastor, church planter, and teacher, Dr. John is known for trustworthy, expositional teaching that lets God's Word speak for itself—verse by verse, without adding personal spin. He makes ancient truth pulse with relevance for today, helping believers from teens to seasoned saints discover fresh insight and practical application. Through daily broadcasts, video series, and books, Dr. John's teaching transforms how you read your Bible and live out your faith.

sessions.title: The Multiverse and the Theory That Explains it All
sessions.subtitle: Reaching the Minds and Hearts of our Students Through Meta Modernism
sessions.description: When Phil began his career there was a lot of talk about our students being a product of the post-modern age. That is not longer true. Today’s students live in a virtual multiverse. Because of this they believe many ideas are true, but what distinguishes them from their relativistic elders is that they also believe some things are true for everyone at all times. Come to this session to understand how they can hold to these contradictions. Discover their deepest desires in this world. More than this, learn how to use this emerging worldview as a powerful springboard to gospel centred thinking and living.

additionalInfo.heading: WiFi Access
additionalInfo.body:
WiFi Access

FREE public WiFi is available at the conference.

Network Name: Northview.Public

additionalInfo.heading: Got Questions?
additionalInfo.body:
Got Questions?

During the main session Friday night and the first main session Saturday morning, you can submit your questions via Slido.com. Join the session with the code #2169 027.

additionalInfo.heading: The AC Podcast
additionalInfo.body:
The AC Podcast

Apologetics Canada has a weekly podcast program where Andy, Steve, Wes, and Troy offer a healthy dose of Christian perspective on cultural issues of our day. Search for The AC Podcast on your favourite podcast platform!

additionalInfo.heading: Donations
additionalInfo.body:
Donations

Apologetics Canada Ministries is a registered Canadian charity that depends on the generous support of people like you. If you appreciate our ministry, please prayerfully consider supporting us financially.

additionalInfo.heading: Apologetics Canada (AC) Table
additionalInfo.body:
Apologetics Canada (AC) Table

Come see us at the AC Table. We'd love to talk to you about what we are up to: the work we are doing, the events and resources that might be right for you, or any questions you might have. We are also looking for more partnering churches as ministry partners in new cities across Canada. Or perhaps you are interested in joining our financial support team because you see the value of this ministry or you have personally been impacted by it. Come visit us.

additionalInfo.heading: Pre-order Conference Recordings
additionalInfo.body:
Pre-order Conference Recordings

All sessions from the conference are being recorded. You can pre-order the recordings as a digital download at the Apologetics Canada book table in the foyer. The recording package contains videos of the Main Sessions and audio files of the Breakout Sessions.

additionalInfo.heading: Can I Trust the Bible?
additionalInfo.body:
Sign up today to receive the video downloads for free!

The Bible is the most important book of the last two millennia. From sitting at the formation of governments, cultures, and law, this collection of ancient documents has shaped the modern world in ways you may have never realized. However, while the Bible's influence has remained, central attitudes towards it have radically shifted. Once referred to as 'the Good Book,' there has been a growing skepticism and downright distaste for the Bible.

Are there good reasons to take the Bible seriously? Can we have confidence that it hasn't been altered, adapted, influenced, and edited since its last book was penned nearly two thousand years ago? What are the implications if the Bible's claims are true?

Sign up today to receive the download links! Click the button below to get started!
